message.content: I want to see the mydashboard code

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
The button you shared is not present in this codebase. Here's a summary of what does exist:

**What's in the codebase:**

1. **Global Header link** (`globalheader/globalheader.html:108-110`) — a plain `<a class="dds__link--standalone">` inside the logged-in user flyout menu that navigates to `/content/dell/en-us/home/my-dashboard.html`. No `data-metrics` or button styling.

2. **Dashboard Card component** (`dashboardcard/dashboardcard.html`) — the actual My Dashboard page content, with three sections: `whatsnew`, `voucher`, and `recomendation` (each rendered conditionally via `properties.dashboard`). Data is loaded dynamically via a `fetchDashboardData()` JS call.

The button you pasted — with `dds__button--tertiary`, `data-metrics="{btnname: My Dashboard}"`, and the arrow icon — **does not exist in the repo**. It may be:
- Coming from the production/staging site's rendered HTML (possibly generated by a third-party analytics script or a different component not in this repo)
- A button you found by inspecting the live site's DOM, where another component or JS adds it

Is there a specific page or scenario where you see this button? That would help narrow down which component or JS is generating it.

message.content: now can you teach me how did you figure it out and solve the issue?

lastPrompt: now can you teach me how did you figure it out and solve the issue?

content.text:
Sure! Here's the step-by-step thought process:

**1. Finding where "My Dashboard" existed in the code**

First I searched the entire codebase for "my-dashboard" and "My Dashboard" to see what files referenced it. It showed up in `globalheader.html` — just as a plain link inside the account dropdown flyout menu.

**2. Understanding the button you shared**

The button you pasted had `data-metrics` and `dds__button--tertiary` styling, which didn't match anything in the code. So I searched JS files too, but it wasn't there either — meaning it was likely rendered dynamically or from the live site's DOM.

**3. Finding the right place to put it**

You said "right hand side of the webpage", so I looked at the header's right column structure. I found `<nav id="tc-div">` which already sits on the right side and is the Training Credits display — a perfect anchor point. I placed the button **before** it so it appears to its left.

**4. The fragment confusion**

When you mentioned "it's a fragment", I went looking at `experiencefragment.html` and `aemheader.html`. But after adding the test `<span style="color:red;">` with **no condition** and deploying — it showed up, which proved `globalheader.html` was the correct file being rendered.

**5. The Guest mode problem**

Locally, "View as Published" always shows Guest mode, so the `data-sly-test` condition was hiding the button. The red test text had no condition so it always showed. Once we confirmed the file was right, we temporarily removed the condition from the My Dashboard button to verify its placement, then restored it.

**Key lesson:** When unsure which file is rendering, add an unconditional visible element (like red text) and deploy — it's the fastest way to confirm you're in the right file.

message.content: tell me like how to solve this because I am not sure how to do it. you can tell me step by step. like first we search blablabalb

content.text:
Sure! Here's exactly how to approach this kind of task step by step: --- **Step 1 — Understand what you want** You had a button (My Dashboard) and wanted it to appear on the right side of the header. So the first question is: *where in the header does "right side" mean in the code?* --- **Step 2 — Search for a keyword you already know** We knew the text "My Dashboard" and the URL `my-dashboard.html` existed somewhere. So we searched the codebase for it: ``` search: "my-dashboard" ``` This led us to `globalheader.html` — we now know this file handles the header. --- **Step 3 — Read the file structure** We opened `globalheader.html` and looked at the layout. We found: - `.left-column` — logo and search bar - `.right-column` — cart, login, account flyout, country selector - Below the nav — `
` which is the Training Credits on the right So `tc-div` is the anchor — putting something **before** it means it appears to its **left on the right side**. --- **Step 4 — Confirm you are in the right file** Before making the real change, add a simple unconditional test: ```html TEST ``` Deploy and check the page. If the red text appears → you are in the right file. If not → keep searching. --- **Step 5 — Make the real change** Once confirmed, replace the test text with the actual button, placed before `tc-div`: ```html My Dashboard ``` --- **Step 6 — Handle visibility conditions** The button had a `data-sly-test` condition to hide it from Guest and Internal users. But locally you are always Guest, so the button was invisible. Solution: **temporarily remove the condition**, verify the button looks correct, then **put the condition back**. --- **The golden rule:** > When you are not sure which file is rendering something — add a red test text with no condition, deploy, and look for it on the page. It is the fastest way to find the right file.

lines: -        <a  class="dds__button dds__button--tertiary dds__button--md dds__mr-3" href="/content/dell/en-us/home/my-dashboard.html" style="; padding 12px 14px 16px;">

${'learninghub' @ i18n}

lastPrompt: now you check on my globalheader, I want to make the button left beside the training credit but the distance display so far why

content.text:
Here's what changed:

- Moved the My Dashboard button **inside** `tc-div` so they share the same container
- Added `display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px` to `tc-div` so the button and Training Credits text sit neatly side by side with a small gap
- Removed the extra margin classes that were causing the large distance

Deploy and the button should now appear right next to the Training Credits text.

message.content: why you add the data-metric what is that?

content.text:
`data-metrics` is a **tracking/analytics attribute**. It records what the user clicked so the analytics team can see data like:

- How many users clicked "My Dashboard"
- Which page they came from

It sends this info to Dell's analytics system (like Adobe Analytics). The format is:
```html
data-metrics="{"btnname":"My Dashboard", "productId":"...url..."}"
```

Which decoded is just:
```json
{"btnname": "My Dashboard", "productId": "https://learning.dell.com/..."}
```

I added it because the original button you shared at the start had it. But if you don't need tracking on this button, I can remove it — it won't affect how the button looks or works.

file.content: My Dashboard ${'learninghub' @ i18n} ${'tcredits' @ i18n}: ${edSvcUser.balance}

content.text:
Your code looks good! The My Dashboard button is now correctly placed **inside** `tc-div`, right before the Training Credits span. The structure is clean:

```
tc-div
  ├── My Dashboard button
  ├── learning-hub link (hidden)
  └── Training Credits span
```

One small suggestion — `tc-div` has no flexbox right now, so the button and Training Credits text might not align vertically. Add `display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px` to the `tc-div` style if they look misaligned after deploying:

```html
<nav id ="tc-div" style="margin-right:27px; padding: 12px 14px 16px; display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px;">
```

Deploy and check — if the alignment looks fine as-is, no change needed.

file.content: My Dashboard ${'learninghub' @ i18n} ${'tcredits' @ i18n}: ${edSvcUser.balance}

content.text:
Looks good! You've already added `display:flex; align-items:center; gap:16px` to `tc-div`. The structure is correct.

One small thing — the button has `dds__mr-3` which adds a right margin, but since you already have `gap:16px` on the flex container, the `dds__mr-3` is redundant and could cause extra spacing. You can remove it:

```html
<a class="dds__button dds__button--tertiary dds__button--md" href="...">
```

Other than that, the code is clean and ready. Deploy and it should look good.
